title: Modal Dialog
subtitle: Overlay modal dialogs via CDK Overlay with full focus management, animations, minimization, and theme integration

basic.title: Basic confirm dialog
basic.description: Open a modal dialog using ModalDialogService.open() or the convenience helpers. The service provides options for title, message, confirm/cancel labels, color, and semantic type.

semanticTypes.title: Semantic types
semanticTypes.description: Use semantic helpers to quickly open pre-styled dialogs: openInfo(), openWarning(), openSuccess(), and openError().

verification.title: Verification dialog
verification.description: Require the user to type a specific phrase before confirming a destructive action. Use openVerification() or set verificationText in the config.

loader.title: Loader modal
loader.description: Display a non-closeable loading spinner overlay during long-running operations. Use openLoader() and close the reference when the operation completes.

dynamicComponent.title: Dynamic component
dynamicComponent.description: Open a modal with a custom Angular component as the body. Inject MODAL_DIALOG_DATA to receive data and MODAL_DIALOG_REF to control the modal from within.
dynamicComponent.seeCode: See the code examples below to integrate a custom component.

minimizable.title: Minimizable modal
minimizable.description: Allow users to minimize the modal to a dock chip at the bottom of the screen without losing context. Requires a unique id in the config.
minimizable.note: A unique id is required when minimizable: true. Calling open() with the same id will restore the minimized modal instead of creating a new one.

timeout.title: Timeout with progress bar
timeout.description: Automatically close the modal after a configurable duration. A visual progress bar tracks the remaining time and can be paused on mouse hover.

footerActions.title: Footer actions
footerActions.description: Customize the modal footer using customButtons, the ModalDialogActionsService (from inside the dynamic component), or the [nuiModalDialogFooter] directive.

statusBar.title: Status bar
statusBar.description: Add a colored border stripe to one side of the modal to convey additional semantic information using the statusBar config option.

footer-custom.title: Custom footer actions
footer-custom.description: Customize the modal footer with your own buttons and templates.
footer-custom.note: Customize the modal footer using customButtons, the ModalDialogActionsService (from inside the dynamic component), or the [nuiModalDialogFooter] directive.

child-footer-actions.title: Actions from child component
child-footer-actions.description: Customize the modal footer from a dynamic component loaded inside the modal.
child-footer-actions.note: Inject ModalDialogActionsService into your dynamic component to register footer buttons from within the component. Actions support reactive disabled and loading states via getters — no update() calls needed.
child-footer-actions.note2: Alternatively, use the nuiModalDialogFooter directive inside a dynamic component for full footer control with native HTML. Unlike ModalDialogActionsService, which registers button data rendered by the modal shell, the directive projects a complete ng-template: any Angular binding re-evaluates on every change detection cycle automatically, without calling update().

html-content.title: HTML content
html-content.description: Pass an HTML string via htmlContent for rich body content without creating a full Angular component. The string is sanitized by Angular’s DomSanitizer.
html-content.note: Use htmlContent for static or server-rendered markup. For Angular bindings (events, interpolation, directives), use bodyTemplate instead.

body-template.title: Body template
body-template.description: Pass an Angular TemplateRef via bodyTemplate to use Angular bindings inside the modal body. Pair with templateContext to inject dynamic data.
body-template.note: The template is rendered with *ngTemplateOutlet. Use let-varName="key" in the template to access values from templateContext.

prevent-close.title: Prevent close
prevent-close.description: Use preventClose to intercept close attempts and conditionally block them. Supports both synchronous and async guards. Subscribe to closePrevented() to react when a close is blocked.
prevent-close.note: The preventClose function must return true (allow) or false / Promise<false> (block). It runs on every close trigger: ESC, backdrop click, and the X button.

backdrop.title: Backdrop & close triggers
backdrop.description: Control the backdrop visibility and which user actions can close the modal using hasBackdrop, closeOnBackdropClick, and closeOnEscape.
backdrop.note: Setting hasBackdrop: false removes the overlay entirely, allowing interaction with the page behind the modal.

update-ref.title: Hot updates via ModalDialogRef
update-ref.description: Use ref.updateTitle(), ref.updateHeaderTemplate(), and ref.updateFooterTemplate() to update an open modal in place without closing and reopening it.
update-ref.note: The title update is signal-based and re-renders synchronously — it works correctly even when called outside Angular’s zone (e.g. from setTimeout).

service.title: ModalDialogService
service.description: Inject ModalDialogService to open and manage modals. All methods return a ModalDialogRef.

config.title: ModalDialogConfig
config.description: Configuration object passed to open(). Properties are merged with the global defaults from provideNUI().

ref.title: ModalDialogRef
ref.description: The reference returned by open(). Use it to close the modal, listen to events, and observe lifecycle changes.

tokens.title: Injection tokens
tokens.description: Use these tokens to inject modal context inside a dynamic component loaded by the service.

custom-button.title: ModalDialogCustomButton
custom-button.description: Inline button definition passed via customButtons in the config. Evaluated once at open time — use ModalDialogActionsService for reactive states.

modal-action.title: ModalDialogAction
modal-action.description: Footer action registered from a dynamic component via ModalDialogActionsService. Supports reactive disabled and loading via getter functions.

actions-service.title: ModalDialogActionsService
actions-service.description: Injected by the dynamic component to register footer actions programmatically. A fresh instance is created per modal by ModalDialogService.

footer-directive.title: ModalDialogFooterDirective
footer-directive.description: Directive applied to an ng-template inside the dynamic component. Projects the full template into the modal footer with native Angular bindings.

colors.title: Color tokens
colors.description: Override these CSS custom properties to adjust modal dialog colors for your brand or custom theme.

layout.title: Layout tokens
layout.description: Control dimensions, spacing, typography, and layer (z-index) of the modal dialog via these CSS variables.

dock.title: Dock tokens
dock.description: Customize the appearance of chip items in the minimized modal dock.

keys.title: Translation tokens
keys.description: The Modal Dialog component uses its own tokens for its texts.
keys.note: All user-facing text in Modal Dialog can be overridden globally using provideNuiI18n(). Others can be translated by passing the custom translation in the open() configuration.

roles.title: ARIA roles and attributes
roles.description: The modal shell automatically applies the required ARIA attributes for screen reader compatibility.

focus.title: Focus management
focus.description: The CDK FocusTrap ensures keyboard navigation is confined to the modal while it is open.
focus.note: Focus is automatically restored to the element that triggered the modal when it closes.

options.title: Global config options
options.description: Default values applied to every modal opened with ModalDialogService. Override per-modal by passing config to open().

example.title: Usage example
example.description: Configure global defaults in your app.config.ts using provideNUI().
